disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Small, sharply defined bright-white patches on the backs of both hands, roughly mirror-image in position
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the white patches?
Where exactly are they and are they on both sides?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're small and really white, like the colour's totally gone, and they're on the backs of both my hands in about the same spots on each side.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No itch, no pain and no visible scaling, flaking or peeling of the patches
trigger_question_examples: Do the patches itch, hurt, or flake at all?
Is there any scale or peeling on the white areas?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, they don't itch or hurt at all, and there's no flaking or peeling on the white bits — the surface looks smooth.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Redness and soreness of the surrounding skin came on tonight after vigorous washing with hot water and harsh soaps/sanitisers
trigger_question_examples: Have you been doing anything to the skin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been scrubbing my hands over and over with really hot water and strong soap and sanitiser all night, and now the skin around the spots is red and stinging.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe acute anxiety with a panic attack and obsessive hand-washing driven by fear of a contagious organism
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling in yourself about this?
What's worrying you most right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm terrified — I had a full panic attack, my heart was racing, and I can't stop washing my hands because I'm sure something is eating the colour out of my skin and it'll spread.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no feeling unwell, no spreading warmth or swelling of the hands
trigger_question_examples: Any fever, or do you feel unwell in yourself?
Are your hands hot, swollen or spreading redness beyond where you scrubbed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, I don't feel sick otherwise, and my hands aren't swollen or hot — just the sore red skin where I scrubbed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The white patches have no sensory loss — normal feeling to touch within them (leprosy screening; cannot be confirmed without in-person sensation testing)
trigger_question_examples: Do the white patches feel normal when you touch them, or numb?
Can you feel light touch and temperature over them the same as the rest of your skin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They feel completely normal when I touch them — not numb at all, same as the rest of my skin.
